title: Brain Neural Underpinnings of Interoception and Decision-Making in Alzheimer's Disease: A Narrative Review.

assets.abstract: This study reviews recent literature on interoception directing decision-making in Alzheimer's disease (AD). According to the somatic marker hypothesis, signals from the internal body direct decision-making and involve the ventromedial prefrontal cortex (vmPFC). After reviewing relevant studies, we summarize the brain areas related to interoception and decision-making (e.g., vmPFC, hippocampus, amygdala, hypothalamus, anterior cingulate cortex, and insular cortex) and their roles in and relation

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.
